title: Dark Matter Communication in the AI Era
summary: Third-year project communicating dark matter to five different audiences, from a tabloid article to a children's comic, with a reflection on where AI helps science communication and where it falls short.

title: Why Imaginary Numbers Matter in Quantum Mechanics
summary: A talk given at the QUIRK+ 2026 Student Research Conference on why complex numbers are unavoidable in quantum theory, drawing on my research award work on Wick rotation and imaginary-time evolution.

title: Schrödinger's Equation: A Wave Equation or Heat Equation?
summary: A short research report asking whether the Schrödinger equation is truly a wave equation or closer to the diffusion equation for heat, and how a rotation to imaginary time turns one into the other.

title: Lab Report on Magnetoresistance
summary: Spin-valve GMR sensor matched theoretical predictions with accurate sensitivity and linearity, while multilayer GMR showed deviations from calibration.
